name: Alex Chen — The Integration Architect
summary: A Senior DevOps Engineer at a fintech company (50-200 employees) who lives in the API docs and integration settings. Alex's primary driver is automating infrastructure workflows end-to-end, and they are quickly frustrated by anything that requires manual intervention or lacks solid API coverage.

location_signals: fintech hub, likely US or UK

goals: Automate CI/CD pipelines using the project management API to eliminate manual status updates
Integrate the tool deeply with GitHub so that PR events automatically update project tasks
Build custom dashboards that surface deployment metrics and sprint progress in one view
Configure webhooks to push real-time events to internal Slack channels and monitoring systems
pains: GraphQL endpoint has rough edges that require workarounds in automation scripts
Setting up team permissions and invites is a manual bottleneck that slows onboarding
Terraform setup requires significant time investment before seeing results
motivations: Reduce toil: every manual step in a workflow is a risk and a time sink
Prove infrastructure-as-code discipline to the wider engineering org
objections: If the GraphQL API doesn't mature, I'll have to maintain fragile REST workarounds forever
decision_triggers: Sees a GitHub integration demo that handles edge cases they've hit in production
API changelog shows GraphQL schema improvements
Colleague recommends the tool after successfully automating their own workflow
sample_quotes: Your REST API is solid but the GraphQL endpoint has some rough edges. Plans to improve the schema?
I need the webhook to fire on task status changes, not just creation — otherwise I'm polling.
If I can't script it, it doesn't scale.

journey_stages.mindset: Testing API coverage and integration depth against internal requirements
key_actions: Sets up webhook config in sandbox

journey_stages.mindset: Building first real automation; hitting limitations and filing feedback

source_evidence.claim: Senior DevOps Engineer at a fintech company (50-200 employees)
GraphQL endpoint has rough edges that require workarounds
Setting up team permissions is a manual bottleneck
Terraform setup requires significant time investment
Automate CI/CD pipelines using the project management API
Integrate deeply with GitHub so PR events update project tasks
Build custom dashboards for deployment and sprint metrics
Configure webhooks to push real-time events to internal systems
Reduce toil: every manual step is a risk and time sink — drives long API setup sessions
Prove infrastructure-as-code discipline through Terraform usage
Worried about GraphQL API maturity blocking automation
